description: Phase 2 of Learning Curation: Implement `curate enrich` to backfill missing metadata (applies_to_files, applies_to_task_types, applies_to_errors, tags) on existing learnings using LLM analysis. Also extends EditLearningParams for task_types/errors (FR-006). Depends on Phase 1 (migration v8, retired_at filters) being merged first.

priorityPhilosophy.description: The hierarchy of what matters most when implementing tasks
hierarchy: 1. PLAN - Anticipate edge cases. Tests verify boundaries work correctly
2. FUNCTIONING CODE - Pragmatic, reliable code that works according to the plan
3. CORRECTNESS - Code compiles, type-checks, passes all tests deterministically
4. CODE QUALITY - Clean code, good patterns, no warnings
principles: Ship solid working code first, then use tests to improve it
Test the boundaries and exceptions - edge cases are where bugs hide
Handle Option/Result explicitly; avoid unwrap() in production code

globalAcceptanceCriteria.description: These criteria apply to ALL implementation tasks
criteria: Rust: No warnings in `cargo check` output
Rust: No warnings in `cargo clippy` output
Rust: All tests pass with `cargo test`
No breaking changes to existing APIs unless explicitly required

priorityGuidelines.medium: 21-50: Fix in current phase if time permits

userStories.title: Initial tests for EditLearningParams extension (FR-006)
userStories.description: Write tests BEFORE implementation that verify add_task_types, remove_task_types, add_errors, remove_errors work through edit_learning(). Tests will fail until FEAT-001 implements the extension.
acceptanceCriteria: Test: add_task_types adds entries to applies_to_task_types JSON column
Test: remove_task_types removes entries from applies_to_task_types
Test: add_errors adds entries to applies_to_errors JSON column
Test: remove_errors removes entries from applies_to_errors
Test: adding to a NULL field creates the array (not crash)
Test: removing from a NULL field is a no-op (not crash)
Test: duplicate add is idempotent (no duplicates in array)
Test: has_updates() returns true when any new field is set
Known-bad discriminator: test that adding task_types does NOT overwrite existing applies_to_files (verifies independent field handling)
Test file compiles (tests may be #[ignore] or expected to fail until FEAT-001)
userStories.notes: Follow the existing test pattern in src/learnings/crud/ or add tests alongside existing ones. The add/remove pattern mirrors add_files/remove_files exactly. Tests depend on Phase 1 (migration v8) being merged — the applies_to_task_types and applies_to_errors columns already exist in the schema.
edgeCases: Add task_types to learning where applies_to_task_types is NULL — should create new JSON array, not crash
Remove task_type from NULL field — should be no-op, not error
Add duplicate task_type — should not create duplicate entries in array
Remove task_type that doesn't exist in array — should be no-op
Add empty string as task_type — should it be filtered out or allowed? (filter out)
invariants: edit_learning with only add_task_types set must not modify any other field
edit_learning with only add_errors set must not modify any other field
has_updates() == false when all fields are None (including new fields)
Existing applies_to_files content is preserved when editing task_types or errors

failureModes.cause: JSON deserialization of NULL column into Vec<String>
Serialization of empty vec back to DB
failureModes.expectedBehavior: Store as NULL (not empty JSON array '[]') to maintain NULL-means-unset semantics

userStories.title: Initial tests for enrich metadata query and field filter
userStories.description: Write tests for the query that finds active learnings with missing metadata. Must handle --field filter, retired_at exclusion, and already-enriched learning exclusion. Tests will fail until FEAT-003 implements the query.
acceptanceCriteria: Test: query returns learnings where applies_to_files IS NULL
Test: query returns learnings where applies_to_task_types IS NULL
Test: query returns learnings where applies_to_errors IS NULL
Test: query excludes retired learnings (retired_at IS NOT NULL)
Test: query excludes fully-enriched learnings (all 3 fields populated)
Test: --field=applies_to_files restricts to learnings missing only that field
Test: 0 matching learnings returns empty vec (no error)
Known-bad discriminator: query with --field=applies_to_files must NOT return a learning that has applies_to_files set but applies_to_task_types NULL
Test file compiles (tests expected to fail until FEAT-003)
userStories.notes: Use in-memory SQLite with schema+migrations applied (including v8 from Phase 1). Create learnings with various metadata states to test the query. The query should use OR logic when no --field filter is set (any NULL field qualifies), and specific field check when --field is set.
edgeCases: All learnings already enriched (all fields populated) — return empty, no LLM call
Learning with some fields populated and others NULL — only NULL fields need enrichment
Retired learning with NULL metadata — must be excluded from enrichment candidates
Learning with empty JSON array '[]' vs NULL — treat '[]' as populated, only NULL needs enrichment
invariants: Query never returns retired learnings regardless of metadata state
Query never returns learnings where the filtered field is already populated
Query returns learnings sorted by ID for deterministic batching

failureModes.cause: SQL treats empty string '' same as NULL
failureModes.expectedBehavior: Use IS NULL check, not = '' — the columns store JSON or NULL

userStories.title: Initial tests for enrich LLM prompt building and response parsing
userStories.description: Write tests for the enrich prompt construction (injection protection, batch formatting) and response parser (best-effort JSON, ID validation, graceful degradation). Tests will fail until FEAT-004 and FEAT-005 implement the prompt/parser.
acceptanceCriteria: Test: prompt includes random UUID delimiter wrapping learning content
Test: prompt includes UNTRUSTED warning for injection protection
Test: prompt includes learning ID, title, content, existing tags for each batch item
Test: prompt requests JSON response with specific field names
Test: parser handles valid JSON array with metadata suggestions
Test: parser handles non-JSON garbage response (returns empty, no crash)
Test: parser handles markdown code block wrapped JSON
Test: parser validates learning IDs against input batch (rejects hallucinated IDs)
Known-bad discriminator: parser must reject a response that references learning ID 999 when batch only contains IDs [1, 2, 3]
Test file compiles (tests expected to fail until FEAT-004/FEAT-005)
userStories.notes: Follow the exact test patterns from src/learnings/ingestion/extraction.rs (test_build_extraction_prompt_*, test_parse_*). The enrich prompt is different from extraction prompt but uses the same security patterns.
edgeCases: LLM returns empty array [] — valid, means no enrichment suggestions
LLM returns metadata for only some learnings in batch — apply what's there, skip rest
LLM suggests overwriting existing metadata — handler (FEAT-006) filters these out; parser passes raw proposals through
LLM returns learning ID that doesn't exist in batch — skip that entry with warning
LLM returns non-array JSON (object instead of array) — graceful degradation to empty
invariants: Parser never crashes regardless of LLM response content
Parser never returns suggestions for non-existent learning IDs
Prompt instructs LLM to skip populated fields; handler (FEAT-006) enforces NULL-only writes as final gate
Prompt always contains injection protection delimiter

failureModes.cause: LLM returns malformed JSON with trailing commas
failureModes.expectedBehavior: serde_json rejects it; parser falls back to empty result with warning
Parse what's there; no size limit on response parsing

failureModes.cause: claude binary not found or not executable
failureModes.expectedBehavior: spawn_claude returns IoError; handler increments llm_errors and continues to next batch

userStories.title: FR-006: Extend EditLearningParams with task_types and errors fields
userStories.description: Add add_task_types, remove_task_types, add_errors, remove_errors to EditLearningParams. Update edit_learning() to handle these fields using the same add/remove pattern as applies_to_files. Update has_updates(). Extend the CLI EditLearning command with corresponding flags. Wire new fields through main.rs dispatch.
acceptanceCriteria: EditLearningParams has 4 new Optional<Vec<String>> fields: add_task_types, remove_task_types, add_errors, remove_errors
has_updates() checks all 4 new fields
edit_learning() handles add_task_types/remove_task_types on applies_to_task_types column (same pattern as files)
edit_learning() handles add_errors/remove_errors on applies_to_errors column (same pattern as files)
Empty result vec stored as NULL (not '[]') to preserve NULL-means-unset semantics
CLI EditLearning has --add-task-types, --remove-task-types, --add-errors, --remove-errors flags
main.rs dispatch passes new fields from CLI to EditLearningParams
All TEST-INIT-001 tests pass
Existing edit_learning tests still pass
userStories.notes: This is a mechanical extension — copy the add_files/remove_files pattern exactly for task_types and errors. The columns applies_to_task_types and applies_to_errors already exist in the schema and store JSON arrays (Option<Vec<String>>). The Learning model struct already has these fields.

correctness: Empty vec after add/remove must be stored as NULL, not '[]' — preserves enrich query semantics (IS NULL means needs enrichment)
Deserialization of NULL column must default to empty vec (unwrap_or_default), not error
New fields must not interfere with existing file/tag operations
performance: Single UPDATE per field group (task_types, errors) — not per-item
style: Follow exact pattern from applies_to_files handling in update.rs lines 86-118
CLI flags use value_delimiter=',' for comma-separated lists (same as --add-files)

userStories.title: Add Enrich variant to CurateAction and create enrich types
userStories.description: Add CurateAction::Enrich variant to the CLI with --dry-run, --batch-size, --field flags. Create EnrichParams, EnrichResult, EnrichProposal, and EnrichFieldFilter types in the curate module.
acceptanceCriteria: CurateAction::Enrich variant in cli/commands.rs with: --dry-run (bool), --batch-size (usize, default 20), --field (optional string)
--field accepts: applies_to_files, applies_to_task_types, applies_to_errors (tags enrichment is always included, not separately filterable)
EnrichParams struct with dry_run, batch_size, field_filter fields in curate types
EnrichResult struct with: dry_run, field_filter (Option<String>), total_candidates, batches_processed, learnings_enriched, llm_errors, proposals (Vec<EnrichProposal>) — derives Serialize
EnrichProposal struct with: learning_id, learning_title, proposed_files, proposed_task_types, proposed_errors, proposed_tags — derives Serialize
EnrichFieldFilter enum (or Option<String>) for --field validation
task-mgr curate enrich --help shows correct flags and descriptions
userStories.notes: Phase 1 created CurateAction with Retire and Unretire variants. Add Enrich to the same enum. Types go in curate/types.rs alongside existing RetireResult/UnretireResult. The --field flag should validate against known field names at parse time (clap value_parser or manual validation).

correctness: All result types must derive Serialize for JSON output mode
Default batch_size must be 20 (PRD FR-004)
--field must only accept valid field names
style: Follow existing CurateAction variant patterns from Phase 1 (Retire, Unretire)
Use clap after_help with usage examples for enrich subcommand

userStories.title: Enrich query: find active learnings with missing metadata
userStories.description: Implement the query that finds active (non-retired) learnings with NULL applicability fields, optionally filtered by --field. Returns learnings sorted by ID for deterministic batching.
acceptanceCriteria: Query function accepts Connection and optional field filter
Without --field: returns learnings where ANY of applies_to_files, applies_to_task_types, applies_to_errors IS NULL
With --field: returns learnings where ONLY the specified field IS NULL
Excludes retired learnings (AND retired_at IS NULL)
Returns full learning data needed for LLM prompt (id, title, content, outcome, existing tags, existing metadata)
Results sorted by id ASC for deterministic batching
All TEST-INIT-002 tests pass
userStories.notes: This is a straightforward SQL query. For tag loading, either JOIN against learning_tags or load tags in a second query. The query should be in src/commands/curate/enrich.rs or a dedicated query module. Consider whether to load tags here or batch-load them separately.

correctness: Must filter retired_at IS NULL — otherwise enriching retired learnings wastes LLM calls
Must use IS NULL (not = '' or = 'null') for metadata field checks
With --field=applies_to_files, must NOT return learnings that already have applies_to_files set
performance: ORDER BY id ASC for deterministic batching across re-runs
style: Return a Vec of a lightweight struct with only the fields needed for prompt building

userStories.title: Enrich LLM prompt building with injection protection
userStories.description: Build the prompt that presents a batch of learnings to the LLM and asks it to infer missing metadata. Must use random delimiter injection protection and clearly instruct the LLM on the expected JSON response format.
acceptanceCriteria: build_enrich_prompt() accepts a batch of learnings with their existing metadata/tags
Prompt uses random UUID delimiter wrapping untrusted learning content (same pattern as extraction.rs)
Prompt includes UNTRUSTED warning text (same pattern as extraction.rs)
Prompt presents each learning with: id, title, content, outcome, existing tags, existing metadata fields
Prompt clearly states which fields are already populated (LLM should skip those)
Prompt requests JSON array response with: learning_id, applies_to_files, applies_to_task_types, applies_to_errors, tags
Prompt includes guidance on what good metadata looks like (file paths as globs, task types as prefixes like FEAT-/FIX-)
All prompt-related TEST-INIT-003 tests pass
userStories.notes: Follow the exact prompt building pattern from src/learnings/ingestion/extraction.rs:15-65. Key differences: (1) input is a batch of existing learnings not raw output, (2) response is metadata suggestions not new learnings, (3) must indicate which fields are already set. Put this in src/commands/curate/enrich.rs.

correctness: Random delimiter MUST use uuid::Uuid::new_v4() — predictable delimiters enable injection
Learning content is UNTRUSTED — must be wrapped in delimiters with warning
Prompt must instruct LLM to only suggest values for NULL fields, never overwrite existing
style: Follow extraction.rs prompt structure exactly: system instruction, field spec, injection warning, delimiter-wrapped content
Use r# raw string for multi-line prompt template

userStories.title: Enrich LLM response parsing with validation and graceful degradation
userStories.description: Parse the LLM's JSON response containing metadata suggestions. Validate learning IDs against the input batch, handle malformed responses gracefully. Returns raw proposals — the handler (FEAT-006) is responsible for filtering out already-populated fields.
acceptanceCriteria: parse_enrich_response() accepts raw LLM output string and a set of valid learning IDs
Handles raw JSON array, markdown code block wrapped JSON (same as extraction.rs)
Parses into Vec<EnrichProposal> with learning_id, proposed metadata fields
Validates each learning_id exists in the input batch (rejects hallucinated IDs with warning)
Returns empty Vec on parse failure (no crash, logs warning to stderr)
Skips entries with invalid/missing learning_id
All parser-related TEST-INIT-003 tests pass
userStories.notes: Reuse extract_json_array() and find_matching_bracket() from src/learnings/ingestion/extraction.rs. Either import them (if pub) or copy the logic. The key difference from extraction parsing: enrich response has learning_id field that must be validated, and proposals are metadata additions not full learnings.

correctness: Hallucinated learning IDs must be rejected — LLM may reference IDs not in the batch
Parse failure must never crash — return empty Vec with stderr warning
Must handle serde_json errors gracefully (malformed JSON, wrong types)
style: Follow graceful degradation pattern from extraction.rs:73-105
Log warnings to stderr with specific details (which ID was invalid, what parse error occurred)

userStories.title: Convert EnrichProposal to EditLearningParams (NULL-only fields)
userStories.description: Implement a pure function that takes a learning's current metadata state and an EnrichProposal, and returns an EditLearningParams with only the NULL fields populated. This is the correctness-critical piece that ensures enrichment never overwrites existing metadata.
acceptanceCriteria: proposal_to_edit_params() takes learning's current metadata (applies_to_files, applies_to_task_types, applies_to_errors, has_tags) and an EnrichProposal
Returns EditLearningParams with add_files set ONLY if learning's applies_to_files is NULL
Returns EditLearningParams with add_task_types set ONLY if learning's applies_to_task_types is NULL
Returns EditLearningParams with add_errors set ONLY if learning's applies_to_errors is NULL
Returns EditLearningParams with add_tags set from proposal (tags are always additive, not overwriting)
Returns None/empty if no NULL fields match the proposal (nothing to enrich)
Function is pure (no side effects, no DB access) — unit-testable in isolation
userStories.notes: This is intentionally a small, focused function. It isolates the correctness-critical 'never overwrite' logic into a pure function that can be thoroughly unit-tested. The orchestration handler (FEAT-008) calls this for each proposal.

correctness: Must never overwrite existing metadata — check learning's current state before populating EditLearningParams fields
NULL means unset (needs enrichment); non-NULL means set (skip, even if empty array '[]')
Tags are always additive — add_tags set regardless of existing tags
style: Pure function with no side effects — takes data in, returns data out
Use Option types to represent 'nothing to enrich' vs 'has updates'

userStories.title: Enrich command handler: batch loop with per-batch commits
userStories.description: Implement the main curate_enrich() function that orchestrates the full workflow: query candidates, batch them, call LLM per batch, convert proposals via FEAT-006, apply via edit_learning(), commit per batch, print progress.
acceptanceCriteria: curate_enrich() accepts Connection and EnrichParams
Queries candidates using FEAT-003 query
Short-circuits with empty result if 0 candidates (no LLM call)
Batches candidates into groups of batch_size
For each batch: builds prompt (FEAT-004), calls spawn_claude(), parses response (FEAT-005)
For each valid proposal: calls proposal_to_edit_params (FEAT-006) then edit_learning()
Each batch is committed in its own transaction (savepoint pattern or explicit BEGIN/COMMIT)
Prints batch progress to stderr: 'Processing batch 2/15...'
dry_run=true shows proposals without applying (no edit_learning calls, no transactions)
spawn_claude failure for one batch doesn't abort remaining batches (continues with error count)
Returns EnrichResult with totals and per-learning proposals
userStories.notes: Uses FEAT-006 (proposal_to_edit_params) for the correctness-critical conversion, keeping the orchestration focused on flow control. Key design: (1) Use edit_learning() for updates via FEAT-006 conversion. (2) Per-batch transactions. (3) Best-effort: continue on LLM errors.

correctness: Per-batch transactions: completed batches survive if process killed between batches
Re-run after interruption: query naturally excludes already-enriched learnings (idempotent)
spawn_claude errors must not lose already-committed batch results
performance: Per-batch transaction commits (not one giant transaction)
Print batch progress to stderr for user feedback
Short-circuit: if 0 candidates, return immediately without LLM call
style: Use edit_learning() CRUD function, not raw SQL, for applying metadata
Follow spawn_claude error handling pattern from ingestion/mod.rs
Log errors and continue (best-effort), don't abort on first failure

userStories.title: Output formatting and main.rs dispatch for curate enrich
userStories.description: Add format_enrich_text() to curate output, add impl_text_formattable! for EnrichResult, wire CurateAction::Enrich dispatch in main.rs.
acceptanceCriteria: format_enrich_text() in curate/output.rs produces human-readable summary
Dry-run output shows each proposal with learning ID, title, and proposed metadata
Non-dry-run output shows summary: N learnings enriched, M batches, K errors
impl_text_formattable!(EnrichResult, format_enrich_text) in handlers.rs
CurateAction::Enrich match arm in main.rs: acquires lock, opens connection, calls curate_enrich, outputs result
JSON output mode works (--format json)
Text output mode works (default)
userStories.notes: Follow the exact pattern from Phase 1's retire/unretire wiring in main.rs and handlers.rs. The format function should be comprehensive for dry-run (show all proposals) but concise for actual runs (show summary). LockGuard needed since enrich writes to DB.

correctness: LockGuard must be acquired before calling curate_enrich (writes to DB)
Must import EnrichResult and format function in handlers.rs
style: Follow existing curate output formatting pattern from Phase 1 (format_retire_text, format_unretire_text)
Dry-run output should be detailed (each proposal), actual run output should be summary

userStories.title: Review implementation for quality, security, and integration wiring
userStories.description: Review all Phase 2 implementation for code quality, error handling, LLM security (injection protection), and verify all new code is fully integrated.
acceptanceCriteria: No unwrap() in production code paths
All errors properly propagated with context
Security: enrich prompt uses random delimiter injection protection
Security: LLM response parsing validates learning IDs (no hallucinated ID injection)
Quality dimensions met: each task's qualityDimensions constraints satisfied
Edge case coverage: each edgeCases entry on TEST-INIT tasks has a corresponding test
New enrich module exported from commands/curate/mod.rs
EnrichResult imported and wired in handlers.rs
main.rs dispatch covers CurateAction::Enrich
edit_learning handles new task_types/errors fields correctly
No dead code warnings for new code
Any issues found have corresponding CODE-FIX-xxx tasks added to JSON
userStories.notes: Critical review. Key security concern: enrich sends learning content to LLM — must verify injection protection. Key correctness concern: enrich must never overwrite existing metadata. For each issue found: 1) Add CODE-FIX-xxx task (priority 14-16), 2) Add to MILESTONE-1 dependsOn, 3) Commit JSON changes.

userStories.title: Review implementation for refactoring opportunities
userStories.description: Analyze Phase 2 implementation code for DRY violations, complexity, coupling, and maintainability issues. Pay special attention to code shared between extraction.rs and enrich.rs (JSON parsing, prompt patterns).
acceptanceCriteria: No code duplication — especially between extraction.rs parsing and enrich parsing
Functions under 30 lines (flag complex ones, especially curate_enrich handler)
No tight coupling between enrich module and internal query details
Code is easy to extend (Phase 3 dedup will use similar LLM patterns)
Any issues found have corresponding REFACTOR-1-xxx tasks added to JSON
userStories.notes: Key refactoring opportunity: extract_json_array() and find_matching_bracket() from extraction.rs might be reused by enrich parsing. If duplicated, suggest extracting to a shared LLM parsing utility. For each issue: 1) Add REFACTOR-1-xxx (priority 18-19), 2) Add to MILESTONE-1 dependsOn, 3) Commit JSON.

userStories.title: Extract shared LLM JSON parsing utilities (extract_json_array, find_matching_bracket)
userStories.description: extract_json_array() and find_matching_bracket() are duplicated verbatim between extraction.rs (lines 108-176) and enrich.rs (lines 370-434). The enrich.rs copy even acknowledges this in a comment. Phase 3 dedup will likely need the same parsing, making this a 3-way duplication risk. Extract both functions to a shared module (e.g. src/llm/json_parsing.rs or src/utils/json.rs), make pub(crate), and import in both extraction.rs and enrich.rs.
acceptanceCriteria: extract_json_array and find_matching_bracket exist in exactly one location (shared module)
extraction.rs imports and uses the shared functions
enrich.rs imports and uses the shared functions
All existing tests pass unchanged
cargo check + clippy + fmt clean
userStories.notes: ~65 lines of identical code. Both functions are private in extraction.rs, private in enrich.rs. Move to a shared location and make pub(crate). Consider placing under src/llm/ since these are LLM response parsing utilities, or under src/utils/ if a more generic location is preferred.

userStories.title: Extract batch processing loop from curate_enrich into helper function
userStories.description: curate_enrich() in enrich.rs is ~105 lines (193-298), well over the 30-line guideline. The batch loop body (lines 234-287) handles LLM call, error recovery, parsing, title backfill, and transaction application. Extract this into a process_single_batch() helper to improve readability and testability.
acceptanceCriteria: curate_enrich() is under 40 lines (orchestration only)
New helper function handles single-batch processing
All existing tests pass unchanged
cargo check + clippy + fmt clean
userStories.notes: The function is already partially decomposed (build_batch_items, apply_proposals_in_transaction). The remaining batch loop body could be extracted to process_single_batch(conn, batch_items, dry_run) -> (Vec<EnrichProposal>, usize enriched, usize errors). Note: edit_learning's repetitive field handling (3x identical add/remove/serialize pattern) is pre-existing and intentionally explicit — not worth abstracting for 3 uses.

userStories.title: Phase 2 implementation complete: enrich working, FR-006 done
userStories.description: Gate checkpoint: all initial tests pass, implementation reviewed, refactored, and fully wired in.
acceptanceCriteria: All initial tests (TEST-INIT-001, TEST-INIT-002, TEST-INIT-003) pass
All implementation tasks (FEAT-001 through FEAT-008) pass
CODE-REVIEW-1 passes (and any spawned CODE-FIX-xxx tasks)
REFACTOR-REVIEW-1 passes (and any spawned REFACTOR-1-xxx tasks)
All new code is reachable from production entry points (no dead code)
No unused warnings for new code in cargo check/cargo clippy
cargo test passes with no failures
cargo fmt --check passes
task-mgr curate enrich --dry-run executes without error
task-mgr edit-learning --add-task-types and --add-errors flags work
userStories.notes: Initial tests pass, implementation reviewed, refactored, and fully wired in.

userStories.title: Comprehensive tests for FR-006 EditLearningParams extension
userStories.description: Expand test coverage for the new EditLearningParams fields: concurrent edits, JSON serialization round-trips, interaction with existing fields, boundary values.
acceptanceCriteria: Test: edit_learning with all 4 new fields set simultaneously
Test: add_task_types + remove_task_types in same call (remove first, then add)
Test: add_errors with very long error patterns (stress test)
Test: round-trip: add task_types → read learning → verify applies_to_task_types populated → remove → verify NULL
Test: JSON output of EditLearningResult includes task_types and errors in updated_fields list
Test: edit_learning with only new fields set (no existing fields) works correctly
80%+ coverage for new code in update.rs
userStories.notes: These tests expand on TEST-INIT-001 with more comprehensive scenarios. Focus on interaction between old and new fields.

userStories.title: Comprehensive tests for enrich handler: error handling, batching, interruption safety
userStories.description: Expand test coverage for the enrich command handler: LLM error recovery, batch boundary behavior, dry-run vs actual, progress output, resume after interruption.
acceptanceCriteria: Test: enrich with 0 candidates returns immediately (no LLM call)
Test: enrich with candidates < batch_size processes in single batch
Test: enrich with candidates > batch_size processes in multiple batches
Test: LLM returns error for one batch but other batches succeed (partial success)
Test: dry-run shows proposals but database unchanged (verify with SELECT after)
Test: re-run after partial completion only processes remaining learnings (already-enriched excluded by query)
Test: enrich with --field filter only enriches specified field
Test: output formatting for dry-run (detailed per-proposal) vs actual (summary)
Test: if one batch's edit_learning() call fails, previous batch results are committed and durable
Test: proposal_to_edit_params correctly skips already-populated fields (unit test the pure function)
80%+ coverage for enrich.rs
userStories.notes: For LLM-dependent tests, consider mocking spawn_claude or testing the prompt/parse functions directly. The handler test may need to mock the LLM call. Focus on the batch loop logic, transaction boundaries, and error recovery. Batch failure durability can be tested by simulating a failed edit_learning call after successful batches.

userStories.title: Review test code for refactoring opportunities
userStories.description: Analyze test code for duplication, helper extraction opportunities, and clarity improvements.
acceptanceCriteria: No duplicated test setup code (helper functions for creating learnings with various metadata states)
Helper functions extracted for common patterns (e.g., create_learning_with_metadata, assert_learning_metadata)
Test names clearly describe what is tested
Any issues found have corresponding REFACTOR-2-xxx tasks added to JSON
userStories.notes: For each issue: 1) Add REFACTOR-2-xxx (priority 40-44), 2) Add to MILESTONE-2 dependsOn, 3) Commit JSON.

userStories.title: Full test coverage achieved for Phase 2
userStories.description: Gate checkpoint: comprehensive tests pass, test code refactored.
acceptanceCriteria: All comprehensive tests (TEST-001, TEST-002) pass
REFACTOR-REVIEW-2 passes (and any spawned REFACTOR-2-xxx tasks)
80%+ coverage on new code (enrich.rs, update.rs additions)
cargo test passes with no failures
No flaky tests

userStories.title: Integration verification: end-to-end curate enrich workflow
userStories.description: Verify the complete enrich workflow works end-to-end: create learnings with missing metadata, run enrich --dry-run, verify no changes, run enrich, verify metadata populated. Also verify FR-006 works through CLI.
acceptanceCriteria: E2E test: create learnings with NULL applies_to_task_types/errors → run curate enrich --dry-run → verify DB unchanged → verify proposals shown
E2E test: run curate enrich → verify applies_to_task_types/errors populated on candidates
E2E test: re-run curate enrich → verify 0 candidates (all already enriched)
E2E test: create mix of retired and active learnings → enrich only processes active
E2E test: --field=applies_to_files only enriches that field
E2E test: edit-learning --add-task-types FEAT-,FIX- works through CLI
E2E test: edit-learning --add-errors 'timeout' works through CLI
Verify JSON and Text output modes for enrich
Verify curate --help shows enrich subcommand with correct flags
userStories.notes: This requires an actual LLM call OR a mock. If mocking, use a fake claude binary that returns predetermined JSON. If calling real LLM, use a small batch. Test in a temp directory with task-mgr init.

userStories.title: Final refactoring review for maintainability
userStories.description: Comprehensive review of all Phase 2 code for DRY violations, complexity hotspots, coupling issues, and code clarity. Ensure Phase 3 (dedup) can build on this cleanly.
acceptanceCriteria: No code duplication across entire implementation and tests
All functions under 30 lines or documented exceptions
Clear separation of concerns: query, prompt, parse, apply
